curriculum.subject: Computer Science
curriculum.overallDescription: 6 units and 43 topics extracted from the syllabus.
coreObjectives: Develop a basic understanding of computer system architecture including hardware components (CPU, memory units), input/output devices, and storage hierarchy.
Explain the distinction between system software, application software, and programming tools including language translators.
Describe the functions of an operating system and its user interface capabilities.
Apply Boolean logic principles including truth tables, De Morgan's laws, and basic logic circuit design (NOT, AND, OR, NAND, NOR, XOR).
Perform conversions between Binary, Octal, Decimal, and Hexadecimal number systems.
Identify and explain encoding schemes such as ASCII, ISCII, Unicode (UTF8, UTF32) for character representation.
Apply computational thinking by analyzing problems, developing algorithms using flowcharts/pseudocode, coding, testing, and debugging solutions.
Demonstrate familiarity with the Python environment including execution modes (interactive/script), character sets, tokens, variables, and comments.
Identify and utilize various data types in Python: integers, floating point numbers, complex numbers, booleans, sequences (string, list, tuple), None, and mappings (dictionary).
Differentiate between mutable and immutable data structures and apply appropriate operations for each.
Utilize arithmetic, relational, logical, assignment, identity, and membership operators with correct precedence rules to evaluate expressions.
Implement error handling strategies by identifying syntax errors, logical errors, and run-time errors in Python programs.

Introduction to Python, Features of Python, executing a simple 'hello world' program, execution modes: interactive mode and script mode, Python character set, Python tokens( keyword, identifier, literal, operator, punctuator), variables, concept of l-value and r-value, use of comments
Number(integer, floating point,complex), boolean, sequence(string, list, tuple), None, Mapping(dictionary), mutable and immutable data types.
arithmetic operators, relational operators, logical operators, assignment operators, augmented assignment operators, identity operators (is, is not), membership operators (in not in)
precedence of operators, expression, evaluation of an expression, type-conversion (explicit and implicit conversion), accepting data as input from the console and displaying output.
syntax errors, logical errors, and run-time errors
introduction, use of indentation, sequential flow, conditional and iterative flow
if, if-else, if-elif-else, flowcharts, simple programs: e.g.: absolute value, sort 3 numbers and divisibility of a number.
for loop, range(), while loop, flowcharts, break and continue statements, nested loops, suggested programs: generating pattern, summation of series, finding the factorial of a positive number, etc.

introduction, string operations (concatenation, repetition, membership and slicing), traversing a string using loops, built-in functions/methods–len(), capitalize(), title(), lower(), upper(), count(), find(), index(), endswith(), startswith(), isalnum(), isalpha(), isdigit(), islower(), isupper(), isspace(),lstrip(), rstrip(), strip(), replace(), join(), partition(), split()

introduction, indexing, list operations (concatenation, repetition, membership and slicing), traversing a list using loops, built-in functions/methods–len(), list(), append(), extend(), insert(), count(), index(), remove(), pop(), reverse(), sort(), sorted(), min(), max(), sum(); nested lists, suggested programs: finding the maximum, minimum, mean of numeric values stored in a list; linear search on list of numbers and counting the frequency of elements in a list.

introduction, indexing, tuple operations (concatenation, repetition, membership and slicing); built-in functions/methods – len(), tuple(), count(), index(), sorted(), min(), max(), sum(); tuple assignment, nested tuple; suggested programs: finding the minimum, maximum, mean of values stored in a tuple; linear search on a tuple of numbers, counting the frequency of elements in a tuple.

introduction, accessing items in a dictionary using keys, mutability of a dictionary (adding a new term, modifying an existing item), traversing a dictionary, built-in functions/methods – len(), dict(), keys(), values(), items(), get(), update(), del, clear(), fromkeys(), copy(), pop(), popitem(), setdefault(), max(), min(), sorted(); Suggested programs: count the number of times a character appears in a given string using a dictionary, create a dictionary with names of employees, their salary and access them.

Importing module using 'import <module>' and using from statement, importing math module (pi, e, sqrt(), ceil(), floor(), pow(), fabs(), sin(), cos(), tan()); random module (random(), randint(), randrange()), statistics module (mean(), median(), mode()).

topics: Familiarization with the basics of Python programming
Expressions, statement, type conversion, and input/output
subtopics: Steps for Problem-solving (Analyzing the problem, developing an algorithm, coding, testing, and debugging), representation of algorithms using flowchart and pseudocode, decomposition

Introduction to Python, Features of Python, executing a simple 'hello world' program, execution modes: interactive mode and script mode, Python character set, Python tokens( keyword, identifier, literal, operator, punctuator), variables, concept of l-value and r-value, use of comments
Number(integer, floating point,complex), boolean, sequence(string, list, tuple), None, Mapping(dictionary), mutable and immutable data types.
arithmetic operators, relational operators, logical operators, assignment operators, augmented assignment operators, identity operators (is, is not), membership operators (in not in)
precedence of operators, expression, evaluation of an expression, type-conversion (explicit and implicit conversion), accepting data as input from the console and displaying output.
syntax errors, logical errors, and run-time errors
introduction, use of indentation, sequential flow, conditional and iterative flow
if, if-else, if-elif-else, flowcharts, simple programs: e.g.: absolute value, sort 3 numbers and divisibility of a number.
for loop, range(), while loop, flowcharts, break and continue statements, nested loops, suggested programs: generating pattern, summation of series, finding the factorial of a positive number, etc.

introduction, string operations (concatenation, repetition, membership and slicing), traversing a string using loops, built-in functions/methods–len(), capitalize(), title(), lower(), upper(), count(), find(), index(), endswith(), startswith(), isalnum(), isalpha(), isdigit(), islower(), isupper(), isspace(),lstrip(), rstrip(), strip(), replace(), join(), partition(), split()

introduction, indexing, list operations (concatenation, repetition, membership and slicing), traversing a list using loops, built-in functions/methods–len(), list(), append(), extend(), insert(), count(), index(), remove(), pop(), reverse(), sort(), sorted(), min(), max(), sum(); nested lists, suggested programs: finding the maximum, minimum, mean of numeric values stored in a list; linear search on list of numbers and counting the frequency of elements in a list.

introduction, indexing, tuple operations (concatenation, repetition, membership and slicing); built-in functions/methods – len(), tuple(), count(), index(), sorted(), min(), max(), sum(); tuple assignment, nested tuple; suggested programs: finding the minimum, maximum, mean of values stored in a tuple; linear search on a tuple of numbers, counting the frequency of elements in a tuple.

introduction, accessing items in a dictionary using keys, mutability of a dictionary (adding a new term, modifying an existing item), traversing a dictionary, built-in functions/methods – len(), dict(), keys(), values(), items(), get(), update(), del, clear(), fromkeys(), copy(), pop(), popitem(), setdefault(), max(), min(), sorted(); Suggested programs: count the number of times a character appears in a given string using a dictionary, create a dictionary with names of employees, their salary and access them.

Importing module using 'import <module>' and using from statement, importing math module (pi, e, sqrt(), ceil(), floor(), pow(), fabs(), sin(), cos(), tan()); random module (random(), randint(), randrange()), statistics module (mean(), median(), mode()).

practicals.title: Lab Test
practicals.unit_name: Unit 2: Computational Thinking and Programming - I
practicals.details: Python program (60% logic + 20% documentation + 20% code quality). Marks: 12.

practicals.title: Report File + Viva
practicals.unit_name: Unit 2: Computational Thinking and Programming - I
practicals.details: Minimum 20 Python programs in report file. Viva voce.

practicals.title: Python Program (Welcome Message)
practicals.unit_name: Unit 2: Computational Thinking and Programming - I
practicals.details: Input a welcome message and display it.

practicals.title: Python Program (Larger/Smaller Number)
practicals.unit_name: Unit 2: Computational Thinking and Programming - I
practicals.details: Input two numbers and display the larger/smaller number.

practicals.title: Python Program (Three Numbers)
practicals.unit_name: Unit 2: Computational Thinking and Programming - I
practicals.details: Input three numbers and display the largest/smallest number.

practicals.title: Python Program (Patterns)
practicals.unit_name: Unit 2: Computational Thinking and Programming - I
practicals.details: Generate patterns using nested loops.

practicals.title: Python Program (Series Summation)
practicals.unit_name: Unit 2: Computational Thinking and Programming - I
practicals.details: Input value of x and n to print sum of series.

practicals.title: Python Program (Perfect/Armstrong/Palindrome)
practicals.unit_name: Unit 2: Computational Thinking and Programming - I
practicals.details: Determine whether a number is perfect, Armstrong, or palindrome.

practicals.title: Python Program (Prime/Composite)
practicals.unit_name: Unit 2: Computational Thinking and Programming - I
practicals.details: Check if a number is prime or composite.

practicals.title: Python Program (Fibonacci)
practicals.unit_name: Unit 2: Computational Thinking and Programming - I
practicals.details: Display terms of a Fibonacci series.

practicals.title: Python Program (GCD/LCM)
practicals.unit_name: Unit 2: Computational Thinking and Programming - I
practicals.details: Compute GCD and LCM of two integers.

practicals.title: Python Program (String Analysis)
practicals.unit_name: Unit 2: Computational Thinking and Programming - I
practicals.details: Count vowels, consonants, uppercase, lowercase characters in a string.

practicals.title: Python Program (Palindrome/Case Conversion)
practicals.unit_name: Unit 2: Computational Thinking and Programming - I
practicals.details: Check if string is palindrome; convert case of characters.

practicals.title: Python Program (List/Tuple Max/Min)
practicals.unit_name: Unit 2: Computational Thinking and Programming - I
practicals.details: Find largest/smallest number in a list/tuple.

practicals.title: Python Program (Swap Elements)
practicals.unit_name: Unit 2: Computational Thinking and Programming - I
practicals.details: Input list of numbers, swap elements at even location with odd location.

practicals.title: Python Program (Search List/Tuple)
practicals.unit_name: Unit 2: Computational Thinking and Programming - I
practicals.details: Input list/tuple, search for a given element.

practicals.title: Python Program (Dictionary Student Marks)
practicals.unit_name: Unit 2: Computational Thinking and Programming - I
practicals.details: Create dictionary with roll number, name, marks; display names of students with marks > 75.

practicals.title: Python Program (Text File Word Display)
practicals.unit_name: Unit 1: Computational Thinking and Programming – 2
practicals.details: Read a text file line by line, display each word separated by #.

practicals.title: Python Program (Text File Character Count)
practicals.unit_name: Unit 1: Computational Thinking and Programming – 2
practicals.details: Read a text file, display number of vowels/consonants/uppercase/lowercase characters.

practicals.title: Python Program (Remove Lines with 'a')
practicals.unit_name: Unit 1: Computational Thinking and Programming – 2
practicals.details: Remove lines containing character 'a' from a file, write to another file.

practicals.title: Python Program (Binary File Search)
practicals.unit_name: Unit 1: Computational Thinking and Programming – 2
practicals.details: Create binary file with name/roll number. Search for roll number, display name or message.

practicals.title: Python Program (Binary File Update)
practicals.unit_name: Unit 1: Computational Thinking and Programming – 2
practicals.details: Create binary file with roll number, name, marks. Input roll number to update marks.

practicals.title: Python Program (Dice Simulation)
practicals.unit_name: Unit 1: Computational Thinking and Programming – 2
practicals.details: Write a random number generator between 1 and 6.

practicals.title: Python Program (Stack Implementation)
practicals.unit_name: Unit 1: Computational Thinking and Programming – 2
practicals.details: Implement a stack using list.

practicals.title: Python Program (CSV Password Search)
practicals.unit_name: Unit 1: Computational Thinking and Programming – 2
practicals.details: Create CSV file with user-id/password, read and search password for given userid.

practicals.title: SQL Query (ALTER Table)
practicals.unit_name: Unit 3: Database Management
practicals.details: Implement ALTER table to add new attributes, modify data type, or drop attribute on student table.

practicals.title: SQL Query (UPDATE Table)
practicals.unit_name: Unit 3: Database Management
practicals.details: Implement UPDATE command to modify data in the student table.

practicals.title: SQL Query (ORDER BY)
practicals.unit_name: Unit 3: Database Management
practicals.details: Display data in ascending/descending order using ORDER BY.

practicals.title: SQL Query (DELETE Tuple)
practicals.unit_name: Unit 3: Database Management
practicals.details: Remove tuple(s) from the student table using DELETE command.

practicals.title: SQL Query (Aggregate Functions)
practicals.unit_name: Unit 3: Database Management
practicals.details: Use GROUP BY to find min, max, sum, count, and average on the student table.

practicals.title: Python-SQL Connectivity Integration
practicals.unit_name: Unit 3: Database Management
practicals.details: Integrate SQL with Python by importing suitable module to perform queries and display data.

projects.title: Class XII Project
projects.details: Create something tangible and useful using Python file handling or Python-SQL connectivity. Work in groups of two to three students on a real-world problem (e.g., GST invoice generator, school software). Avoid plagiarism.

marks_distribution: Class XI: Theory (70), Practical (30). Unit-wise breakdown: Computer Systems and Organisation (Theory: 10, Practical: 10); Computational Thinking and Programming - I (Theory: 45, Practical: 60); Society, Law, and Ethics (Theory: 15, Practical: —)

Class XII: Theory (70), Practical (30). Unit-wise breakdown: Computational Thinking and Programming – 2 (Theory: 40, Practical: 50); Computer Networks (Theory: 10, Practical: —); Database Management (Theory: 20, Practical: 20)
question_paper_design: Class XI Theory: Total 70 marks distributed across units based on syllabus weightage.
Class XII Theory: Total 70 marks distributed across units based on syllabus weightage.
internal_assessment: Lab Test (12 marks for Class XI, 8 marks for Class XII)

project_assessment: Class XI Project: Uses concepts learnt; Marks allocated in practical section.
Class XII Project: Tangible and useful application using Python file handling/Python-SQL connectivity; Group size 2-3 students; Minimum 6 months duration.

units.unit_name: Unit 2: Computational Thinking and Programming - I
topics: Familiarization with the basics of Python programming
Expressions, statement, type conversion, and input/output
subtopics: Steps for Problem-solving (Analyzing the problem, developing an algorithm, coding, testing, and debugging), representation of algorithms using flowchart and pseudocode, decomposition

Introduction to Python, Features of Python, executing a simple 'hello world' program, execution modes: interactive mode and script mode, Python character set, Python tokens( keyword, identifier, literal, operator, punctuator), variables, concept of l-value and r-value, use of comments
Number(integer, floating point,complex), boolean, sequence(string, list, tuple), None, Mapping(dictionary), mutable and immutable data types.
arithmetic operators, relational operators, logical operators, assignment operators, augmented assignment operators, identity operators (is, is not), membership operators (in not in)
precedence of operators, expression, evaluation of an expression, type-conversion (explicit and implicit conversion), accepting data as input from the console and displaying output.
syntax errors, logical errors, and run-time errors
introduction, use of indentation, sequential flow, conditional and iterative flow
if, if-else, if-elif-else, flowcharts, simple programs: e.g.: absolute value, sort 3 numbers and divisibility of a number.
for loop, range(), while loop, flowcharts, break and continue statements, nested loops, suggested programs: generating pattern, summation of series, finding the factorial of a positive number, etc.

introduction, string operations (concatenation, repetition, membership and slicing), traversing a string using loops, built-in functions/methods–len(), capitalize(), title(), lower(), upper(), count(), find(), index(), endswith(), startswith(), isalnum(), isalpha(), isdigit(), islower(), isupper(), isspace(),lstrip(), rstrip(), strip(), replace(), join(), partition(), split()

introduction, indexing, list operations (concatenation, repetition, membership and slicing), traversing a list using loops, built-in functions/methods–len(), list(), append(), extend(), insert(), count(), index(), remove(), pop(), reverse(), sort(), sorted(), min(), max(), sum(); nested lists, suggested programs: finding the maximum, minimum, mean of numeric values stored in a list; linear search on list of numbers and counting the frequency of elements in a list.

introduction, indexing, tuple operations (concatenation, repetition, membership and slicing); built-in functions/methods – len(), tuple(), count(), index(), sorted(), min(), max(), sum(); tuple assignment, nested tuple; suggested programs: finding the minimum, maximum, mean of values stored in a tuple; linear search on a tuple of numbers, counting the frequency of elements in a tuple.

introduction, accessing items in a dictionary using keys, mutability of a dictionary (adding a new term, modifying an existing item), traversing a dictionary, built-in functions/methods – len(), dict(), keys(), values(), items(), get(), update(), del, clear(), fromkeys(), copy(), pop(), popitem(), setdefault(), max(), min(), sorted(); Suggested programs: count the number of times a character appears in a given string using a dictionary, create a dictionary with names of employees, their salary and access them.

Importing module using 'import <module>' and using from statement, importing math module (pi, e, sqrt(), ceil(), floor(), pow(), fabs(), sin(), cos(), tan()); random module (random(), randint(), randrange()), statistics module (mean(), median(), mode()).

practicals.unit_name: Unit 2: Computational Thinking and Programming - I
practicals.details: Minimum 20 Python programs in report file. Viva voce.

practicals.title: Python Program (Welcome Message)
practicals.unit_name: Unit 2: Computational Thinking and Programming - I
practicals.details: Input a welcome message and display it.

practicals.title: Python Program (Larger/Smaller Number)
practicals.unit_name: Unit 2: Computational Thinking and Programming - I
practicals.details: Input two numbers and display the larger/smaller number.

practicals.title: Python Program (Three Numbers)
practicals.unit_name: Unit 2: Computational Thinking and Programming - I
practicals.details: Input three numbers and display the largest/smallest number.

practicals.title: Python Program (Patterns)
practicals.unit_name: Unit 2: Computational Thinking and Programming - I
practicals.details: Generate patterns using nested loops.

practicals.title: Python Program (Series Summation)
practicals.unit_name: Unit 2: Computational Thinking and Programming - I
practicals.details: Input value of x and n to print sum of series.

practicals.title: Python Program (Perfect/Armstrong/Palindrome)
practicals.unit_name: Unit 2: Computational Thinking and Programming - I
practicals.details: Determine whether a number is perfect, Armstrong, or palindrome.

practicals.title: Python Program (Prime/Composite)
practicals.unit_name: Unit 2: Computational Thinking and Programming - I
practicals.details: Check if a number is prime or composite.

practicals.title: Python Program (Fibonacci)
practicals.unit_name: Unit 2: Computational Thinking and Programming - I
practicals.details: Display terms of a Fibonacci series.

practicals.title: Python Program (GCD/LCM)
practicals.unit_name: Unit 2: Computational Thinking and Programming - I
practicals.details: Compute GCD and LCM of two integers.

practicals.title: Python Program (String Analysis)
practicals.unit_name: Unit 2: Computational Thinking and Programming - I
practicals.details: Count vowels, consonants, uppercase, lowercase characters in a string.

practicals.title: Python Program (Palindrome/Case Conversion)
practicals.unit_name: Unit 2: Computational Thinking and Programming - I
practicals.details: Check if string is palindrome; convert case of characters.

practicals.title: Python Program (List/Tuple Max/Min)
practicals.unit_name: Unit 2: Computational Thinking and Programming - I
practicals.details: Find largest/smallest number in a list/tuple.

practicals.title: Python Program (Swap Elements)
practicals.unit_name: Unit 2: Computational Thinking and Programming - I
practicals.details: Input list of numbers, swap elements at even location with odd location.

practicals.title: Python Program (Search List/Tuple)
practicals.unit_name: Unit 2: Computational Thinking and Programming - I
practicals.details: Input list/tuple, search for a given element.

practicals.unit_name: Unit 2: Computational Thinking and Programming - I

practicals.unit_name: Unit 1: Computational Thinking and Programming – 2

practicals.unit_name: Unit 1: Computational Thinking and Programming – 2

practicals.title: Python Program (Remove Lines with 'a')
practicals.unit_name: Unit 1: Computational Thinking and Programming – 2

practicals.unit_name: Unit 1: Computational Thinking and Programming – 2

practicals.unit_name: Unit 1: Computational Thinking and Programming – 2

marks_distribution: Class XI: Theory (70), Practical (30). Unit-wise breakdown: Computer Systems and Organisation (Theory: 10, Practical: 10); Computational Thinking and Programming - I (Theory: 45, Practical: 60); Society, Law, and Ethics (Theory: 15, Practical: —)

Class XII: Theory (70), Practical (30). Unit-wise breakdown: Computational Thinking and Programming – 2 (Theory: 40, Practical: 50); Computer Networks (Theory: 10, Practical: —); Database Management (Theory: 20, Practical: 20)
question_paper_design: Class XI Theory: Total 70 marks distributed across units based on syllabus weightage.
Class XII Theory: Total 70 marks distributed across units based on syllabus weightage.
internal_assessment: Lab Test (12 marks for Class XI, 8 marks for Class XII)
Report File + Viva (10 marks total: Report file minimum programs and Viva voce)
practical_assessment: Class XI Practical Components: Lab Test (Python program logic/documentation/code quality), Report File (Minimum 20 Python programs, Viva Voce)
Class XII Practical Components: Lab Test (Python program + SQL queries on tables), Report File (15 Python programs, 5 sets of SQL Queries, 4 Python-SQL connectivity programs, Viva Voce)
project_assessment: Class XI Project: Uses concepts learnt; Marks allocated in practical section.
Class XII Project: Tangible and useful application using Python file handling/Python-SQL connectivity; Group size 2-3 students; Minimum 6 months duration.

topics: Revision of Python topics covered in Class XI.

Functions: types of function (built-in functions, functions defined in module, user defined functions), creating user defined function, arguments and parameters, default parameters, positional parameters, function returning value(s), flow of execution, scope of a variable (global scope, local scope)
Exception Handling: Introduction, handling exceptions using try-except-finally blocks
Introduction to files, types of files (Text file, Binary file, CSV file), relative and absolute paths
subtopics: Text file: opening a text file, text file open modes (r, r+, w, w+, a, a+), closing a text file, opening a file using with clause, writing/appending data to a text file using write() and writelines(), reading from a text file using read(), readline() and readlines(), seek and tell methods, manipulation of data in a text file

Binary file: basic operations on a binary file: open using file open modes (rb, rb+, wb, wb+, ab, ab+), close a binary file, import pickle module, dump() and load() method, read, write/create, search, append and update operations in a binary file
CSV file: import csv module, open / close csv file, write into a csv file using writer(),writerow(),writerows() and read from a csv file using reader()
Data Structure: Stack, operations on stack (push & pop), implementation of stack using list.

topics: Evolution of networking: introduction to computer networks, evolution of networking (ARPANET, NSFNET, INTERNET)

Data communication terminologies: concept of communication, components of data communication (sender,receiver, message, communication media, protocols), measuring capacity of communication media (bandwidth, data transfer rate), IP address, switching techniques (Circuit switching, Packet switching)
Transmission media: Wired communication media (Twisted pair cable, Co-axial cable, Fiber-optic cable), Wireless media (Radio waves, Micro waves, Infrared waves)
Network devices (Modem, Ethernet card, RJ45, Repeater, Hub, Switch, Router, Gateway, WIFI card)
Network topologies and Network types: types of networks (PAN, LAN, MAN, WAN), networking topologies (Bus, Star, Tree)
Network protocol: HTTP, FTP, PPP, SMTP, TCP/IP, POP3, HTTPS, TELNET, VoIP

subtopics: Steps for Problem-solving (Analyzing the problem, developing an algorithm, coding, testing, and debugging), representation of algorithms using flowchart and pseudocode, decomposition

Introduction to Python, Features of Python, executing a simple 'hello world' program, execution modes: interactive mode and script mode, Python character set, Python tokens( keyword, identifier, literal, operator, punctuator), variables, concept of l-value and r-value, use of comments
Number(integer, floating point,complex), boolean, sequence(string, list, tuple), None, Mapping(dictionary), mutable and immutable data types.
arithmetic operators, relational operators, logical operators, assignment operators, augmented assignment operators, identity operators (is, is not), membership operators (in not in)
precedence of operators, expression, evaluation of an expression, type-conversion (explicit and implicit conversion), accepting data as input from the console and displaying output.
syntax errors, logical errors, and run-time errors
introduction, use of indentation, sequential flow, conditional and iterative flow
if, if-else, if-elif-else, flowcharts, simple programs: e.g.: absolute value, sort 3 numbers and divisibility of a number.
for loop, range(), while loop, flowcharts, break and continue statements, nested loops, suggested programs: generating pattern, summation of series, finding the factorial of a positive number, etc.

introduction, string operations (concatenation, repetition, membership and slicing), traversing a string using loops, built-in functions/methods–len(), capitalize(), title(), lower(), upper(), count(), find(), index(), endswith(), startswith(), isalnum(), isalpha(), isdigit(), islower(), isupper(), isspace(),lstrip(), rstrip(), strip(), replace(), join(), partition(), split()

introduction, indexing, list operations (concatenation, repetition, membership and slicing), traversing a list using loops, built-in functions/methods–len(), list(), append(), extend(), insert(), count(), index(), remove(), pop(), reverse(), sort(), sorted(), min(), max(), sum(); nested lists, suggested programs: finding the maximum, minimum, mean of numeric values stored in a list; linear search on list of numbers and counting the frequency of elements in a list.

introduction, indexing, tuple operations (concatenation, repetition, membership and slicing); built-in functions/methods – len(), tuple(), count(), index(), sorted(), min(), max(), sum(); tuple assignment, nested tuple; suggested programs: finding the minimum, maximum, mean of values stored in a tuple; linear search on a tuple of numbers, counting the frequency of elements in a tuple.

Importing module using 'import <module>' and using from statement, importing math module (pi, e, sqrt(), ceil(), floor(), pow(), fabs(), sin(), cos(), tan()); random module (random(), randint(), randrange()), statistics module (mean(), median(), mode()).

Introduction to Python, Features of Python, executing a simple 'hello world' program, execution modes: interactive mode and script mode, Python character set, Python tokens( keyword, identifier, literal, operator, punctuator), variables, concept of l-value and r-value, use of comments
Number(integer, floating point,complex), boolean, sequence(string, list, tuple), None, Mapping(dictionary), mutable and immutable data types.
arithmetic operators, relational operators, logical operators, assignment operators, augmented assignment operators, identity operators (is, is not), membership operators (in not in)
precedence of operators, expression, evaluation of an expression, type-conversion (explicit and implicit conversion), accepting data as input from the console and displaying output.
syntax errors, logical errors, and run-time errors
introduction, use of indentation, sequential flow, conditional and iterative flow
if, if-else, if-elif-else, flowcharts, simple programs: e.g.: absolute value, sort 3 numbers and divisibility of a number.
for loop, range(), while loop, flowcharts, break and continue statements, nested loops, suggested programs: generating pattern, summation of series, finding the factorial of a positive number, etc.

introduction, string operations (concatenation, repetition, membership and slicing), traversing a string using loops, built-in functions/methods–len(), capitalize(), title(), lower(), upper(), count(), find(), index(), endswith(), startswith(), isalnum(), isalpha(), isdigit(), islower(), isupper(), isspace(),lstrip(), rstrip(), strip(), replace(), join(), partition(), split()

introduction, indexing, list operations (concatenation, repetition, membership and slicing), traversing a list using loops, built-in functions/methods–len(), list(), append(), extend(), insert(), count(), index(), remove(), pop(), reverse(), sort(), sorted(), min(), max(), sum(); nested lists, suggested programs: finding the maximum, minimum, mean of numeric values stored in a list; linear search on list of numbers and counting the frequency of elements in a list.

introduction, indexing, tuple operations (concatenation, repetition, membership and slicing); built-in functions/methods – len(), tuple(), count(), index(), sorted(), min(), max(), sum(); tuple assignment, nested tuple; suggested programs: finding the minimum, maximum, mean of values stored in a tuple; linear search on a tuple of numbers, counting the frequency of elements in a tuple.

introduction, accessing items in a dictionary using keys, mutability of a dictionary (adding a new term, modifying an existing item), traversing a dictionary, built-in functions/methods – len(), dict(), keys(), values(), items(), get(), update(), del, clear(), fromkeys(), copy(), pop(), popitem(), setdefault(), max(), min(), sorted(); Suggested programs: count the number of times a character appears in a given string using a dictionary, create a dictionary with names of employees, their salary and access them.

Importing module using 'import <module>' and using from statement, importing math module (pi, e, sqrt(), ceil(), floor(), pow(), fabs(), sin(), cos(), tan()); random module (random(), randint(), randrange()), statistics module (mean(), median(), mode()).

competency_groups.title: Computer Systems and Organisation Competencies
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to understanding computer architecture, software types, operating systems, logic circuits, number systems, and encoding schemes.
competencies: Develop a basic understanding of computer system architecture including hardware components (CPU, memory units), input/output devices, and storage hierarchy.
Explain the distinction between system software, application software, and programming tools including language translators.
Describe the functions of an operating system and its user interface capabilities.
Apply Boolean logic principles including truth tables, De Morgan's laws, and basic logic circuit design (NOT, AND, OR, NAND, NOR, XOR).
Perform conversions between Binary, Octal, Decimal, and Hexadecimal number systems.
Identify and explain encoding schemes such as ASCII, ISCII, Unicode (UTF8, UTF32) for character representation.

competency_groups.title: Computational Thinking and Programming Competencies (Class XI)
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to problem-solving methodologies, Python programming fundamentals, data types, operators, control structures, and basic data structures.
competencies: Apply computational thinking by analyzing problems, developing algorithms using flowcharts/pseudocode, coding, testing, and debugging solutions.
Demonstrate familiarity with the Python environment including execution modes (interactive/script), character sets, tokens, variables, and comments.
Identify and utilize various data types in Python: integers, floating point numbers, complex numbers, booleans, sequences (string, list, tuple), None, and mappings (dictionary).
Differentiate between mutable and immutable data structures and apply appropriate operations for each.
Utilize arithmetic, relational, logical, assignment, identity, and membership operators with correct precedence rules to evaluate expressions.
Implement error handling strategies by identifying syntax errors, logical errors, and run-time errors in Python programs.

Control program flow using sequential execution, conditional statements (if, if-else, if-elif-else), and iterative structures (for loops, while loops) including nested loops and control break/continue statements.
Manipulate strings effectively using built-in methods for operations like concatenation, slicing, case conversion, searching, and splitting.
Perform list operations including indexing, traversal, modification, sorting, and implementing algorithms like linear search or finding min/max values.
Execute tuple operations similar to lists but respecting immutability constraints.
Manage dictionaries by accessing items via keys, modifying contents, traversing the structure, and utilizing built-in methods for data manipulation.
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit 2: Computational Thinking and Programming - I

competency_groups.title: Society, Law and Ethics Competencies (Class XI)
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to digital etiquette, intellectual property rights, cyber safety, malware awareness, e-waste management, legal frameworks, and social inclusivity.
competencies: Adhere to net etiquettes in communication and social media interactions.
Understand Intellectual Property Rights (copyright, patent, trademark) including violations like plagiarism and infringement, and recognize open source licensing models.
Identify various cyber crimes such as hacking, eavesdropping, phishing, ransomware, cyber trolling, and bullying.
Apply principles of cyber safety including secure browsing practices, identity protection, and maintaining confidentiality online.
Recognize types of malware (viruses, trojans, adware) and their potential impacts on systems.
Implement responsible e-waste management practices regarding the disposal of electronic gadgets.
Understand the provisions of the Information Technology Act (IT Act).
Acknowledge gender and disability issues in the context of teaching and using computers to promote inclusivity.

competency_groups.title: Advanced Programming Competencies (Class XII)
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to advanced Python concepts including functions, exception handling, file operations (text, binary, CSV), and data structures.
competencies: Apply the concept of functions by defining user-defined functions with various parameter types (positional, default) and understanding scope (global/local).
Implement exception handling mechanisms using try-except-finally blocks to manage runtime errors gracefully.
Perform file operations on text files including opening modes, reading/writing data, seeking positions, and managing resources via context managers.
Execute binary file operations using the pickle module for serialization/deserialization of objects (dump/load) and perform read/write/search/update actions.
Handle CSV files by importing modules to write rows/records and read data from structured comma-separated values.
Implement stack data structures including push/pop operations and realize their implementation using Python lists.

competency_groups.title: Computer Networks Competencies (Class XII)
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to networking evolution, data communication principles, transmission media, devices, topologies, protocols, and web services.
competencies: Trace the evolution of computer networks from ARPANET/NSFNET to the modern INTERNET.
Explain components of data communication including sender/receiver roles, message types, media characteristics (bandwidth, transfer rate), IP addressing, and switching techniques (circuit/packet).
Differentiate between wired transmission media (twisted pair, coaxial, fiber-optic) and wireless media (radio waves, microwaves, infrared).
Identify network devices such as modems, repeaters, hubs, switches, routers, gateways, and interface cards.
Analyze network topologies (Bus, Star, Tree) and classify networks by geographical scope (PAN, LAN, MAN, WAN).
Understand the roles of key networking protocols including HTTP, FTP, SMTP, TCP/IP, POP3, HTTPS, TELNET, VoIP, PPP.
Describe web services components including WWW, HTML/XML standards, domain names, URLs, browsers, servers, and hosting concepts.

competency_groups.title: Database Management Competencies (Class XII)
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to database concepts, relational models, SQL operations, and Python-SQL integration.
competencies: Explain fundamental database concepts including the need for databases in data management.
Define elements of the Relational Data Model: relations, attributes, tuples, domains, degree, cardinality, and various key types (candidate, primary, alternate, foreign).
Execute SQL commands using DDL to create/alter/drop databases and tables, manage constraints (not null, unique), and define data types.
Perform Data Manipulation Language (DML) operations including insert, delete, select with complex clauses (where, in, between, order by, distinct, aliasing).
Utilize SQL aggregate functions (max, min, avg, sum, count) combined with group by and having clauses for data analysis.
Implement join operations to combine data from multiple tables using cartesian products, equi-joins, and natural joins.
Establish database connectivity between Python and SQL databases using cursor objects to execute queries (insert, update, delete), fetch results, and manage transactions.

learning_outcomes.unit_name: Unit 2: Computational Thinking and Programming - I
outcomes: Manipulate strings effectively using built-in methods for operations like concatenation, slicing, case conversion, searching, and splitting.

learning_outcomes.unit_name: Unit 2: Computational Thinking and Programming - I
outcomes: Perform list operations including indexing, traversal, modification, sorting, and implementing algorithms like linear search or finding min/max values.

learning_outcomes.unit_name: Unit 2: Computational Thinking and Programming - I
outcomes: Execute tuple operations similar to lists but respecting immutability constraints.

learning_outcomes.unit_name: Unit 2: Computational Thinking and Programming - I
outcomes: Manage dictionaries by accessing items via keys, modifying contents, traversing the structure, and utilizing built-in methods for data manipulation.
Adhere to net etiquettes in communication and social media interactions.
Understand Intellectual Property Rights (copyright, patent, trademark) including violations like plagiarism and infringement, and recognize open source licensing models.
Identify various cyber crimes such as hacking, eavesdropping, phishing, ransomware, cyber trolling, and bullying.
Apply principles of cyber safety including secure browsing practices, identity protection, and maintaining confidentiality online.
Recognize types of malware (viruses, trojans, adware) and their potential impacts on systems.
Implement responsible e-waste management practices regarding the disposal of electronic gadgets.
Understand the provisions of the Information Technology Act (IT Act).
Acknowledge gender and disability issues in the context of teaching and using computers to promote inclusivity.
Apply the concept of functions by defining user-defined functions with various parameter types (positional, default) and understanding scope (global/local).
Implement exception handling mechanisms using try-except-finally blocks to manage runtime errors gracefully.
Perform file operations on text files including opening modes, reading/writing data, seeking positions, and managing resources via context managers.
Execute binary file operations using the pickle module for serialization/deserialization of objects (dump/load) and perform read/write/search/update actions.
Handle CSV files by importing modules to write rows/records and read data from structured comma-separated values.
Implement stack data structures including push/pop operations and realize their implementation using Python lists.
Trace the evolution of computer networks from ARPANET/NSFNET to the modern INTERNET.
Explain components of data communication including sender/receiver roles, message types, media characteristics (bandwidth, transfer rate), IP addressing, and switching techniques (circuit/packet).
Differentiate between wired transmission media (twisted pair, coaxial, fiber-optic) and wireless media (radio waves, microwaves, infrared).
Identify network devices such as modems, repeaters, hubs, switches, routers, gateways, and interface cards.
Analyze network topologies (Bus, Star, Tree) and classify networks by geographical scope (PAN, LAN, MAN, WAN).
Understand the roles of key networking protocols including HTTP, FTP, SMTP, TCP/IP, POP3, HTTPS, TELNET, VoIP, PPP.
Describe web services components including WWW, HTML/XML standards, domain names, URLs, browsers, servers, and hosting concepts.
Explain fundamental database concepts including the need for databases in data management.
Define elements of the Relational Data Model: relations, attributes, tuples, domains, degree, cardinality, and various key types (candidate, primary, alternate, foreign).
Execute SQL commands using DDL to create/alter/drop databases and tables, manage constraints (not null, unique), and define data types.
Perform Data Manipulation Language (DML) operations including insert, delete, select with complex clauses (where, in, between, order by, distinct, aliasing).
Utilize SQL aggregate functions (max, min, avg, sum, count) combined with group by and having clauses for data analysis.
Implement join operations to combine data from multiple tables using cartesian products, equi-joins, and natural joins.
Establish database connectivity between Python and SQL databases using cursor objects to execute queries (insert, update, delete), fetch results, and manage transactions.
